body:
Closes #136 

## Purpose 

--> Ensure inactive sea-ice cells have zero velocity instead of free-drift velocity, so velocities outside active ice do not affect tracer advection, CFL diagnostics, or boundary/halo behavior. 

This PR splits the sea ice momentum into three buckets: 

1) Active sea ice, where behaviour is unchanged; 
2) Marginal sea ice, where free drift velocity is used based on a concentration threshold of `minimum_concentration = 1e-3` and `minimum_mass = 1.0`.
3) 0 sea ice concentration (within machine precision), where zero velocity is used. 

## To-do

--> Add or update focused tests for inactive-cell velocity behavior.

## Content
--> Updated explicit momentum stepping to set u and v to zero when ice mass or concentration are below active thresholds.
--> Updated split-explicit momentum stepping with the same inactive-cell zeroing behavior.
--> Updated momentum equation documentation to state that below-threshold velocities are set to zero.
--> Verified with a small smoke test that both ExplicitSolver and SplitExplicitSolver keep u/v at zero with nonzero configured free drift and zero ice.

<!---

I have:

- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.

title: Zero inactive sea ice velocities
Update citation for ClimaSeaIce version to v0.5.0
